---
title: "First Break AI"
description: "First Break AI is a free, open cohort to learn AI by doing — inference, training, and product building. No prerequisites, just follow the roadmap and build in the open."
title-block-style: none
css: styles/home.css
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-banner">
  <img src="public/images/Fort-Journey-01-hero.jpg" alt="First Break AI" class="hero-bg" loading="eager" decoding="async" fetchpriority="high" />
  <div class="hero-overlay">
    <div class="hero-text">
      <h1 class="hero-title">First Break AI</h1>
      <p class="hero-tagline">Your first break in AI — a guided journey from first commit to capstone</p>
      <p class="hero-sub">Free, open cohort to upskill in training, inference, and AI product building.</p>
      <a href="https://discord.gg/hRPese4H3F" class="hero-journey-btn" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Start Journey, Join Discord &#9654;</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="whats-new-ticker" aria-label="What's new at First Break AI">
  <span class="ticker-label">What's New</span>
  <div class="ticker-viewport">
    <div class="ticker-track">
      <div class="ticker-group">
        <a class="ticker-item" href="lessons/lesson-1-huggingface-beyond-upload.qmd">
          <span class="ticker-pill new">New · Lesson 1</span>
          <span class="ticker-text">HuggingFace Beyond Upload — why an open model is not a file, it's a supply chain you can inspect.</span>
        </a>
        <span class="ticker-sep" aria-hidden="true">●</span>
        <a class="ticker-item" href="office-hours/2026-05-08.qmd">
          <span class="ticker-pill">Office Hours · 8 May</span>
          <span class="ticker-text">Cohort 01 kickoff — three intuitions, HuggingFace deep dive, live pure-C demo.</span>
        </a>
        <span class="ticker-sep" aria-hidden="true">●</span>
      </div>
      <div class="ticker-group" aria-hidden="true">
        <a class="ticker-item" href="lessons/lesson-1-huggingface-beyond-upload.qmd" tabindex="-1">
          <span class="ticker-pill new">New · Lesson 1</span>
          <span class="ticker-text">HuggingFace Beyond Upload — why an open model is not a file, it's a supply chain you can inspect.</span>
        </a>
        <span class="ticker-sep">●</span>
        <a class="ticker-item" href="office-hours/2026-05-08.qmd" tabindex="-1">
          <span class="ticker-pill">Office Hours · 8 May</span>
          <span class="ticker-text">Cohort 01 kickoff — three intuitions, HuggingFace deep dive, live pure-C demo.</span>
        </a>
        <span class="ticker-sep">●</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="journey-preview">
  <div class="journey-preview-track">
    <a href="journey/#scene-1" class="journey-preview-card">
      <span class="journey-preview-num">1</span>
      <span class="journey-preview-label">Ship something real</span>
    </a>
    <a href="journey/#scene-2" class="journey-preview-card">
      <span class="journey-preview-num">2</span>
      <span class="journey-preview-label">See inside the machine</span>
    </a>
    <a href="journey/#scene-3" class="journey-preview-card">
      <span class="journey-preview-num">3</span>
      <span class="journey-preview-label">Think at production scale</span>
    </a>
    <a href="journey/#scene-4" class="journey-preview-card">
      <span class="journey-preview-num">4</span>
      <span class="journey-preview-label">Train your own</span>
    </a>
    <a href="journey/#scene-5" class="journey-preview-card">
      <span class="journey-preview-num">5</span>
      <span class="journey-preview-label">Ship a product</span>
    </a>
    <a href="journey/#scene-6" class="journey-preview-card">
      <span class="journey-preview-num">6</span>
      <span class="journey-preview-label">Prove it</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</div>

<section class="paths-strip" aria-labelledby="paths-headline">
  <div class="paths-strip-head">
    <span class="paths-eyebrow">Start here</span>
    <h2 class="paths-headline" id="paths-headline">Two ways into First Break AI</h2>
    <p class="paths-sub">Pick a path — they're built to work together. Most learners ping-pong between the map and the lessons.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="paths-grid">
    <a href="roadmap.html" class="path-card path-card-roadmap">
      <span class="path-card-step">01 · The map</span>
      <span class="path-card-title">Roadmap</span>
      <span class="path-card-desc">Six steps from first commit to capstone. Know where you're going before you start running.</span>
      <span class="path-card-cta">Open the roadmap <span class="path-card-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</span></span>
    </a>
    <a href="lessons/" class="path-card path-card-lessons">
      <span class="path-card-step">02 · The build</span>
      <span class="path-card-title">Lessons</span>
      <span class="path-card-desc">Hands-on deep dives — HuggingFace, training, inference, and shipping AI-powered products.</span>
      <span class="path-card-cta">Start with Lesson 1 <span class="path-card-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</span></span>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.content-section}

**Cohort: 1 May 2026 — 30 June 2026 (2 months)** [<span class="cohort-live-badge"><span class="cohort-live-dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>Live</span>]{.cohort-live-wrap}

First Break AI is a free, community-driven cohort for anyone who wants their first break in AI. It doesn't matter what you studied or where you work — what matters is that you're ready to learn by doing. We focus on what matters: running and training models, understanding inference, and shipping AI-powered products. Most learning is self-directed and peer-supported; the roadmap, checklist, and resources live in the open so you can contribute and others can follow. The goal is simple: **upskill, build, showcase** — and get that first role or first break in AI.

## Who it's for

You don't need a specific degree. You need a starting point. This cohort is for anyone — students, professionals, career switchers, the simply curious — who wants their first real break in AI. No applications, no prerequisites. Follow the roadmap, build in the open, and let your work speak for itself.

**Cohort lead:** [FireHacker](https://thefirehacker.github.io/) (blog, TIL) · [GitHub @thefirehacker](https://github.com/thefirehacker) · [About](about.qmd)

::: {.cta-banner}
### Ready to start your journey?

This is a free, community-driven cohort. To join, sign up on Discord — that's where the cohort lives, where you get help, and where you connect with fellow learners.

[Join Discord and start learning](https://discord.gg/hRPese4H3F){.cta-button}

**Office Hours:** Every Friday, 9:00 — 10:00 PM IST. Meeting link shared on Discord.
<span class="office-hours-local"></span>

::: {.cta-fineprint}
Recommended for learners aged **16+**. If you are under 18, please join only with parent or guardian permission. By joining you agree to our [Community Guidelines](community-guidelines.qmd), [Terms](terms.qmd), and [Privacy Policy](privacy.qmd).
:::
:::

```{=html}
<div class="cohort-poster" id="cohort-poster-anchor">
  <div class="cohort-poster-header">
    <span class="cohort-poster-tag">Bonus · The Anatomy</span>
    <h2 class="cohort-poster-title">Want the full picture?</h2>
    <p class="cohort-poster-sub">The cohort drawn as one inference flow — six steps, every detail on a single poster. Click to enlarge.</p>
  </div>
  <a class="cohort-poster-link"
     href="public/images/firstbreakai-anatomy-poster.png"
     target="_blank"
     rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open the First Break AI anatomy poster full-size in a new tab">
    <img class="cohort-poster-img"
         src="public/images/firstbreakai-anatomy-poster-1500.webp"
         alt="First Break AI cohort poster — Anatomy of a First Break in AI. Cohort 01, 1 May – 30 June 2026. Six numbered side panels (A–F) covering Step 1 First use of AI for coding, Step 2 Run a model locally, Step 3 Inference deep dive, Step 4 Training fundamentals, Step 5 Build an AI product, Step 6 Capstone or open-source contribution. Central diagram of Qwen3 0.6B inference flow: input → text embeddings → Qwen3 DecoderLayer ×28 → RMSNorm → Linear → output. Cohort ledger at the bottom."
         width="1332"
         height="1868"
         loading="lazy"
         decoding="async" />
  </a>
</div>
```

## Get started

| Section | Description |
|--------|--------------|
| [Lesson 0: Welcome to First Break AI](lessons/lesson-0-welcome.qmd) | Cohort intro video with interactive transcript — start here. Also on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r9uykyGAdJQ&list=PLEzXCZNdmgBm-SbXuXHxn9_vXpvFbrKKL) (full [playlist](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLEzXCZNdmgBm-SbXuXHxn9_vXpvFbrKKL)) |
| [Roadmap](roadmap.qmd) | Learning path: Quarto blog, local inference, training, and beyond |
| [Checklist](checklist.qmd) | Accounts to create (Hugging Face, GitHub, Fetchlens.ai, Colab) and who to follow |
| [AI Setup](setup.qmd) | AI-based IDE (Cursor / Claude Code), ChatGPT, Open Router |

## Frequently asked

```{=html}
<div class="faq">

  <details open class="faq-item">
    <summary>Do I need to know math to take this course?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p><strong>No.</strong> We deliberately go in reverse: start by <em>running</em> a model on your laptop, then add math (attention, RoPE, softmax) only when you have already seen the thing it describes. That is the same teaching approach Andrej Karpathy uses in <em>Let's reproduce GPT-2</em>.</p>
      <p>Lesson 1's section <a href="lessons/lesson-1-huggingface-beyond-upload.qmd#do-you-need-deep-math-for-this">"Do you need deep math for this?"</a> goes deeper. If you have the math, great — it speeds you up. If you don't, what you can't skip is willingness to read code and run experiments.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>Do I need a CS or tech background?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>You need comfort with reading code (any language) and running terminal commands. That's the floor.</p>
      <p>Past learners have come from career switches, design, math/econ/physics, and self-taught backgrounds. There are <strong>no applications and no prerequisites</strong> — the <a href="roadmap.qmd">roadmap</a> and <a href="checklist.qmd">checklist</a> tell you exactly what to install on day one.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>Is this really free? What's the catch?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>Yes, free. No paywall, no upsell, no certificate-for-a-fee. For comparison, three current AI-engineering cohorts that audience-overlapping learners are already paying for:</p>
      <table class="faq-compare">
        <thead>
          <tr><th></th><th>Length</th><th>Time / wk</th><th>Price</th></tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td>Paid cohort A</td><td>4 weeks</td><td>~6 hrs</td><td><strong>$2,000</strong></td></tr>
          <tr><td>Paid cohort B</td><td>8 weeks</td><td>10–12 hrs</td><td><strong>$2,000</strong></td></tr>
          <tr><td>Paid cohort C</td><td>6 weeks</td><td>5+ hrs</td><td>(sold out)</td></tr>
          <tr class="faq-compare-self"><td><strong>First Break AI</strong></td><td><strong>8 weeks</strong></td><td>5–8 hrs</td><td><strong>$0</strong></td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
      <p>Same scope, free, longer cohort window. The catch is you have to show up — the cohort works because <em>you</em> build in public, not because we chase you.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>How much time per week do I need to commit?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>Plan for <strong>5–8 hours a week</strong> to comfortably keep up — one lesson, Friday office hours, and the homework. Comparable paid cohorts ask for 5–12 hours per week, so this is in the same range.</p>
      <p>Many learners spend more by choice; the roadmap goes deeper than the weekly minimum and the code is open. Cohort 01 runs <strong>1 May 2026 – 30 June 2026</strong>, but everything stays online afterward, so you can also move at your own pace.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>What hardware do I need? Mac, Windows, or do I need a GPU?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>Any modern laptop is enough to start. We design the lessons <strong>CPU-first</strong> — for example, <a href="lessons/lesson-1-huggingface-beyond-upload.qmd">Lesson 1</a> runs Qwen3-0.6B in pure C on your laptop's CPU with no GPU required.</p>
      <p>Mac (Apple Silicon or Intel), Windows (with WSL), and Linux all work. When the cohort hits larger training experiments in Step 4, the <a href="setup.qmd">AI Setup</a> page covers free-tier cloud GPU options.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>Will I get a certificate or job placement?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>No certificate, no placement service. What you walk away with is a <strong>portfolio</strong>: a Quarto blog of everything you learned, a model you trained from scratch, a deployed AI product, and optionally a merged open-source contribution.</p>
      <p>In 2026, that portfolio is a much stronger hiring signal than a course PDF. Recruiters can see your blog and your GitHub commits; they can't see a certificate badge.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>What if I miss a session, or join mid-cohort?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>Everything is asynchronous-friendly. Office hours are recorded; AI-generated podcast summaries and lesson recaps go up on <a href="journey/">The Journey</a> within a few days; transcripts are interactive and searchable.</p>
      <p>You can join Cohort 01 today, in three weeks, or after Cohort 01 ends — the materials stay online and the Discord stays active. Nobody is gated.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

  <details class="faq-item">
    <summary>How is this different from fast.ai, Karpathy's lectures, or HuggingFace's free course?</summary>
    <div class="faq-body">
      <p>First Break AI is <strong>openly inspired by Andrej Karpathy's <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l8pRSuU81PU"><em>Let's reproduce GPT-2 (124M)</em></a> and <a href="https://github.com/karpathy/llm.c"><code>llm.c</code></a></strong> — the from-scratch, no-magic, run-it-in-pure-C approach to teaching how LLMs actually work. Those are still the gold standard for a single lecturer building a model in front of you, and they are the philosophical foundation under this entire cohort. The pure-C Qwen3 demo in <a href="lessons/lesson-1-huggingface-beyond-upload.qmd">Lesson 1</a> (<a href="https://github.com/thefirehacker/QWEN3-RunLocally">code</a> by William Song) is the direct intellectual descendant of <code>llm.c</code>.</p>
      <p>We sit on top of that work and extend it in four directions:</p>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>Cohort-based.</strong> Live Friday office hours, 9–10 PM IST, where you can ask anything. Karpathy's lectures are one-way; this is two-way.</li>
        <li><strong>2026-current stack.</strong> Qwen3 instead of GPT-2, <code>transformers</code> v5, vLLM, agent tooling, the HuggingFace supply-chain lens — but the teaching philosophy (run the model first, study the math after) is Karpathy's, applied to today's models.</li>
        <li><strong>Shipping a product is in scope.</strong> Step 5 of the roadmap is building a deployed AI product, not just training a model in a notebook.</li>
        <li><strong>Agent-readable, by design.</strong> The site ships structured data (<code>Course</code>, <code>FAQPage</code>) for AI answer engines, and <a href="lessons/lesson-1-huggingface-beyond-upload.qmd">Lesson 1</a> walks you through <a href="https://huggingface.co/docs/dataset-viewer/croissant">Croissant</a> — the same machine-readable lens, applied to model and dataset releases. Per-lesson manifests are on the roadmap.</li>
      </ul>
      <p>fast.ai and HuggingFace's free course are excellent complementary reads, and the <a href="roadmap.qmd">Roadmap</a> links to them where they fit.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

</div>

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "url": "https://cohort.bubblnet.com/",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I need to know math to take this course?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No. We deliberately go in reverse: start by running a model on your laptop, then add math (attention, RoPE, softmax) only when you have already seen the thing it describes. That is the same teaching approach Andrej Karpathy uses in Let's reproduce GPT-2. Lesson 1 has a deeper section on this. If you have the math, great — it speeds you up. If you don't, what you can't skip is willingness to read code and run experiments."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I need a CS or tech background?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "You need comfort with reading code (any language) and running terminal commands. That's the floor. Past learners have come from career switches, design, math/econ/physics, and self-taught backgrounds. There are no applications and no prerequisites — the roadmap and checklist tell you exactly what to install on day one."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is this really free? What's the catch?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, free. No paywall, no upsell, no certificate-for-a-fee. For comparison, three current AI-engineering cohorts that audience-overlapping learners are already paying for charge between $1,000 and $2,000 USD for 4 to 8 weeks, with 5 to 12 hours per week. First Break AI is 8 weeks, 5 to 8 hours per week, $0. Same scope, free, longer cohort window. The catch is you have to show up — the cohort works because you build in public, not because we chase you."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much time per week do I need to commit?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Plan for 5 to 8 hours a week to comfortably keep up — one lesson, Friday office hours, and the homework. Comparable paid cohorts ask for 5 to 12 hours per week, so this is in the same range. Many learners spend more by choice; the roadmap goes deeper than the weekly minimum and the code is open. Cohort 01 runs 1 May 2026 to 30 June 2026, but everything stays online afterward, so you can also move at your own pace."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What hardware do I need? Mac, Windows, or do I need a GPU?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Any modern laptop is enough to start. We design the lessons CPU-first — for example, Lesson 1 runs Qwen3-0.6B in pure C on your laptop's CPU with no GPU required. Mac (Apple Silicon or Intel), Windows (with WSL), and Linux all work. When the cohort hits larger training experiments in Step 4, the AI Setup page covers free-tier cloud GPU options."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Will I get a certificate or job placement?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No certificate, no placement service. What you walk away with is a portfolio: a Quarto blog of everything you learned, a model you trained from scratch, a deployed AI product, and optionally a merged open-source contribution. In 2026, that portfolio is a much stronger hiring signal than a course PDF. Recruiters can see your blog and your GitHub commits; they can't see a certificate badge."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if I miss a session, or join mid-cohort?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Everything is asynchronous-friendly. Office hours are recorded; AI-generated podcast summaries and lesson recaps go up on The Journey within a few days; transcripts are interactive and searchable. You can join Cohort 01 today, in three weeks, or after Cohort 01 ends — the materials stay online and the Discord stays active. Nobody is gated."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How is this different from fast.ai, Karpathy's lectures, or HuggingFace's free course?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "First Break AI is openly inspired by Andrej Karpathy's Let's reproduce GPT-2 (124M) and llm.c — the from-scratch, no-magic, run-it-in-pure-C approach to teaching how LLMs actually work. Those are still the gold standard for a single lecturer building a model in front of you, and they are the philosophical foundation under this entire cohort. The pure-C Qwen3 demo in Lesson 1 (code by William Song) is the direct intellectual descendant of llm.c. We sit on top of that work and extend it in four directions. First, cohort-based: live Friday office hours where you can ask anything. Second, a 2026-current stack: Qwen3 instead of GPT-2, transformers v5, vLLM, agent tooling, the HuggingFace supply-chain lens. Third, shipping a product is in scope — Step 5 of the roadmap is building a deployed AI product, not just training a model in a notebook. Fourth, agent-readable by design: the site ships structured data (Course, FAQPage) for AI answer engines, and Lesson 1 walks you through Croissant — the same machine-readable lens, applied to model and dataset releases. Per-lesson manifests are on the roadmap. fast.ai and HuggingFace's free course are excellent complementary reads, and the roadmap links to them where they fit."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>
```

::: {.ai-content-notice}
**About AI-generated content:** Office hours transcripts and cohort material are used to create AI-generated podcast lessons, summaries, and other learning resources published on this site and [The Journey](journey/). All learner names are anonymized — no real names are used. By participating in office hours, you consent to your contributions being used in this way.
:::

:::

```{=html}
<script>
(function() {
  var els = document.querySelectorAll('.office-hours-local');
  if (!els.length) return;
  var tz = Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone;
  var fri = new Date();
  fri.setUTCHours(15, 30, 0, 0);
  var end = new Date(fri.getTime() + 3600000);
  var fmt = function(d) {
    return d.toLocaleTimeString([], { hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit', hour12: true });
  };
  var localStart = fmt(fri);
  var localEnd = fmt(end);
  var tzLabel = tz.replace(/_/g, ' ');
  if (tz !== 'Asia/Calcutta' && tz !== 'Asia/Kolkata') {
    els.forEach(function(el) {
      el.textContent = 'That\u2019s ' + localStart + ' \u2013 ' + localEnd + ' in your time (' + tzLabel + ').';
    });
  }
})();
</script>
```
